Many good works have been looked down upon with contempt by proud and haughty people. Those who disagree about almost everything will unite in persecution. Nehemiah did not respond to these fools according to their foolishness, but instead looked up to God through prayer. God’s people have often been a despised people, but he hears all the insults that are put upon them, and it is their comfort that he does so. Nehemiah had reason to think that the hearts of those sinners were desperately hardened; otherwise, he would not have prayed that their sins might never be erased. Good work goes well when people are willing to do it. The reproaches of enemies should motivate us to our duty, not drive us from it.

Hindering good work is what bad men aim at, and expect success in; but good work is God’s work, and it will prosper. God has many ways of bringing to light, and so of thwarting, the plans of his church's enemies. If our enemies cannot frighten us from duty or deceive us into sin, they cannot hurt us. Nehemiah put himself and his cause under divine protection. It was the way of this good man, and should be our way. All his cares, all his griefs, all his fears, he spread before God. Before he used any means, he prayed to God. After praying, he set a watch against the enemy. If we think to secure ourselves by prayer without watchfulness, we are lazy and tempt God; if by watchfulness without prayer, we are proud and disregard God: either way, we forfeit his protection. God’s care for our safety should engage and encourage us to continue with vigor in our duty. As soon as a danger is over, let us return to our work and trust God again.

We must watch always against spiritual enemies, and not expect that our warfare will be over until our work is ended. The word of God is the sword of the Spirit, which we ought to have always at hand, and never have to seek for it, either in our labors or in our conflicts as Christians. Every true Christian is both a laborer and a soldier, working with one hand and fighting with the other. Good work is likely to go on with success when those who labor in it make a business of it. And Satan fears to assault the watchful Christian; or, if attacked, the Lord fights for him. Thus must we wait until the close of life, never putting off our armor until our work and warfare are ended; then we shall be welcomed to the rest and joy of our Lord.
